task.title: Add `codex-review trace` concept for opening or exporting run evidence.

context.problem_statement: The workflow stack captures receipts, summaries, task packets, context packets, and task lifecycle reports, but it does not yet name a single trace concept for opening or exporting the evidence behind an agent run. Define the `codex-review trace` concept so future CLI work can expose run evidence for transcript review and debugging without scattering private transcript handling across prompts.
background: AGW-007 defined the local session receipt schema.
AGW-008 added a concise receipt-summary renderer.
AGW-074 added harness metric fields across receipts and task packets.
AGW-084 added the maintainer queue prompt for active work and owner decisions.
AGW-089 made task closeout evidence part of source task packets.
AGW-092 and AGW-093 added compact context-bundle and deterministic-report routing concepts.
AGW-097 added the companion kit's local agent-state ledger for dirty-state, task, automation, readiness, and receipt evidence.
AGW-029 is explicitly docs first; the backlog records CLI implementation as later.
non_goals: Do not implement a `codex-review trace` CLI command in this slice.
Do not edit repo-contract-kit or installed target-repo command behavior.
Do not change receipt, task-packet, or review schemas unless the docs-first concept cannot be made precise without a small source-side schema note.
Do not export, summarize, or store private Codex transcripts from this task.
Do not add network, hosted model, browser, GitHub API, or account dependencies.
Do not broaden the task into a full observability dashboard, PR bot, or trace database.

report_sources: git ls-remote origin refs/heads/main for source and kit remotes
previous_task_state.notes: AGW-029 packet creation began only after AGW-025 was pushed in both repos, both remotes matched local HEADs, source and kit worktrees were clean, source task-status reported zero active tasks, and agent-next selected AGW-029.

closeout_required_before_start.reason: The previous AGW-025 task has source and kit closeout commits, matching remote refs, clean worktrees, and no active task metadata.
closeout_required_before_start.required_next_step: Proceed with exactly one AGW-029 worker in agent-workflow-kit; refresh task-status if source or kit state becomes dirty before worker launch.

goal_alignment.repo_goal: Keep agent-workflow-kit as the source of portable prompt, schema, receipt, and harness concepts that make local agent work auditable before installed command behavior is added in repo-contract-kit.

area_contracts.purpose: Names harness components, evidence surfaces, metric families, and future quality gates.
Documents the human/operator rhythm for orienting, scoping, executing, and handing off local agent work.
Explains how humans and agents use the source prompt kit and evidence artifacts.
Canonical source for prompts and adapters that may need to reference trace evidence as a future handoff/debugging surface.
Defines existing session evidence; schema changes are protected unless strictly required for the docs-first trace concept.

stop_conditions: Stop if the work requires implementing an installed CLI, target-repo Make target, or repo-contract-kit behavior.
Stop if trace docs would imply transcript export is automatic, remote, or safe to publish without explicit operator choice.
Stop if the concept duplicates receipt-summary, context-packet, or state-ledger responsibilities instead of defining how they compose.
Stop if private transcript content, credentials, account state, or cross-repo user data would be embedded in committed examples.
Stop if prompt adapter edits are made without corresponding canonical prompt source changes and adapter validation.

protected_files: Installed target-repo templates and managed files
Runtime task metadata or sidecar receipts in real target repos
Private transcript content, credentials, cookies, account state, and machine-local history outside explicit local evidence paths
Unrelated backlog rows and unrelated dirty work
Generated prompt adapters without canonical prompt source changes
expected_outputs: A docs-first trace concept that defines what evidence a future `codex-review trace` command should open or export.
Clear relationship between trace, session receipts, receipt summaries, task packets, context packets, task-status, state-ledger, and closeout receipts.
Trace modes or use cases such as inspect/open, export bundle, privacy-redacted handoff, and debugging missing evidence, without implementing CLI behavior.
Privacy and local-only boundaries for transcripts, command output, paths, receipts, and account data.
A small future CLI contract sketch that names inputs, outputs, refusal cases, and validation expectations for a later repo-contract-kit task.
Prompt or maintainer docs updated only if the trace concept needs to be visible in handoff/debugging guidance.
VERSION and CHANGELOG updated if source behavior/docs surface changes are version-impacting.
Source backlog rows and summary close AGW-029 only after validation passes.

acceptance_criteria.description: The source docs define a `codex-review trace` concept for opening/exporting run evidence and make clear that this task does not implement the CLI.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Read the changed docs and confirm they include the name `codex-review trace`, evidence inputs, output shapes, and a CLI-later boundary.

acceptance_criteria.description: The concept composes existing evidence surfaces instead of replacing them: receipts, receipt summaries, task packets, context packets, task-status, state-ledger, and closeout/finalizer receipts.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Changed docs or prompt guidance reference the relevant existing artifacts and explain what the future trace surface would gather from each.

acceptance_criteria.description: Privacy and local-only rules are explicit: no automatic transcript export, no account mutation, no remote upload, and redaction/omission language for sensitive paths or private content.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Docs review confirms privacy boundaries and refusal cases are present.

acceptance_criteria.description: A future CLI contract sketch names inputs, outputs, refusal cases, and validation evidence for a later implementation task without committing current CLI flags as shipped behavior.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Docs review confirms the contract is labeled as future/planned and does not add installed Make targets or scripts.

acceptance_criteria.description: Prompt indexes or maintainer/verification guidance mention trace evidence only where it helps debugging or handoff, and adapters are regenerated if canonical prompts change.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Run prompt adapter checks when prompt files change; otherwise record that no prompt adapter update was needed.

acceptance_criteria.description: The behavior/docs surface change is versioned when required by the repo versioning contract.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Run make version-check and confirm VERSION/CHANGELOG state is accepted.

acceptance_criteria.description: Source backlog rows, split backlog, feature matrix or summary close AGW-029 only after validation passes.

commands.expected_result: pass if workflows/prompts or workflows/manifest.json changed; otherwise note no prompt adapter change

commands.command: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python3 -m unittest tests.test_render_session_receipt_summary tests.test_export_workflow_adapters tests.test_research_workflow_artifacts
commands.expected_result: pass or justify narrower focused tests if implementation remains docs-only

evidence_to_capture: changed docs showing trace concept and CLI-later boundary
prompt adapter check output or explicit no-prompt-change note
focused test output or docs-only test rationale
backlog and split-backlog validation after closeout

readiness_check.command: make agent-task-ready TASK=AGW-029 TASK_READY_JSON=1 or record why unavailable for this parent-run packet
readiness_check.expected_result: ready report passes or blocker is recorded

lifecycle_action.command: make agent-task-finalize TASK=AGW-029 TASK_RECEIPT=<path> TASK_FINALIZE_JSON=1 or lifecycle fallback
lifecycle_action.expected_result: metadata is closed and final receipt is linked, or fallback receipt is durable

final_task_status.expected_result: task is terminal or no local metadata was created and the reason is recorded

closeout_preview.expected_result: eligible, retained, or blocked cleanup state is recorded

closeout_requirements.dirty_state_explanation: Final handoff must state whether the checkout is clean, only expected files are dirty, cleanup is blocked, or unrelated dirt was preserved.

paths: source backlog summary and feature matrix files
docs_impact.notes: This is a docs-first source concept for a future evidence trace interface; docs updates are the primary delivery shape.

known_risks: The concept could over-promise a shipped CLI surface before implementation exists.
Trace examples could accidentally normalize exporting private transcript content.
The docs could duplicate receipt-summary, context-bundle, or state-ledger responsibilities instead of defining composition.
Prompt updates could increase token footprint if they embed long trace examples.
Schema changes are likely unnecessary for a docs-first concept and could create avoidable migration work.
stop_conditions: The implementation requires a new script, Make target, installed kit command, or target-repo behavior.
Docs would imply trace export is remote, automatic, or safe for public sharing by default.
The worker cannot define the concept without private local transcript examples.
Validation requires changing unrelated backlog items or companion-kit files.

approval.notes: User approved implementing the workflow-stack plan and asked to add the dirty-state follow-ons as fully planned backlog items; parent is continuing the approved backlog one item at a time.

overlap_warnings: This is source-side docs/concept work and must not edit /Volumes/Myrtle/Code/04_Code/Hermes/doc-contract-kit/.
Do not turn the concept into a shipped CLI in AGW-029; create a follow-on backlog item if implementation details are ready.
Parent handles source task-packet commit, worker launch, integration, validation, commit, push, and backlog closeout.
coordination.worker_rule: Use exactly one worker for this backlog item. The worker owns source implementation files only; the parent owns the source task-packet commit, integration, validation, and publication.

deterministic_reports: make agent-task-status TASK_STATUS_INCLUDE_CLOSED=1 TASK_STATUS_JSON=1 passed with zero active tasks and no hazards.
make agent-next selected AGW-029 and reported dirty false.
make agent-token-budget passed with 97 files and 77458 estimated tokens.
make kit-status reported installed repo-contract-kit 0.4.41, target repo version 0.2.18, and four managed localizations.

token_budget.guidance: Keep the trace concept compact and reference existing evidence surfaces by name instead of copying large receipt or transcript examples.

handoff.owner: one worker agent for source implementation; parent for packet commit, integration, validation, push, and backlog closeout
handoff.next_packet_hint: After AGW-029 closes, use AGW-031 or another open source-side docs/review item unless AGW-029 produces a separate accepted CLI implementation follow-on.
